content.text: § 115C-240. Authority and duties of State Board of Education....

(d) ...The State Board shall also require local boards to implement the Transportation Information Management System or an equivalent system approved by the State Board of Education, no later than September 1, 1992.
Pre-TIGER Sources: Paper DOT Maps; Paper Municipal Maps; Charlotte DIME file; WCPSS GIS; Riding roads/Manual address range entry

content.description: Two photographs showing people digitizing maps. The left photo shows a person using a digitizing puck on a large paper map mounted on a table. The right photo shows a person in a dress shirt and a woman sitting at a large digitizing table working with a computer screen in the background.
A close-up shot of a pair of hands holding scissors to trim a large paper document being produced by a pen plotter.
A man standing next to a large format pen plotter in an office setting.
A woman standing next to a large format pen plotter holding scissors to trim the output paper.
A screenshot of the Edulog NT software interface showing a GIS workspace map with streets and overlay layers.
content.caption: Edulog nt, Workspace.wsp, GIS Overlays, Autostreet

content.text: Railroads are added to TIMS as non-travel streets
Railroad warnings can be placed within driver directions generated from TIMS and can alert the driver to an upcoming stop.
Load Counts used by NCDOT to prioritize signal enhancements

content.description: A map of a railroad network with various colored symbols representing crossing types, including red triangles, green circles, and blue squares.
content.caption: Red Triangles = Private/Commercial Crossings; Green Circles = State Maintained Crossings; Blue Squares = Bridge Crossings

content.description: Two maps showing bus stop locations. The left map shows numerous yellow bus stops across a street network. The right map shows fewer red 'STOP' icon bus stops across the same network, representing a consolidation of stops.
Two maps side-by-side showing bus routes with multiple small stops on the left and consolidated, fewer stops on the right.
